title: National Maritime Day: India's oil lifeline churns through conflict waters
content: On most days, India's economic heartbeat is invisible. It does not pulse through stock exchanges or policy corridors but across vast, restless waters where ships move quietly between continents. Yet, when conflict erupts in narrow choke points like the Strait of Hormuz, that invisible system becomes the country’s most visible vulnerability. The ongoing tensions have done exactly that, forcing India to confront a reality it has long managed but rarely foregrounded.

title: Meta CTO’s message to engineers and college graduates looking to join technology industry
content: Meta's CTO, Andrew Bosworth, urged aspiring tech professionals to prioritize hands-on building over theoretical study. He emphasized immersing oneself in practical projects, from "vibe coding" with AI tools for software to using Raspberry Pi and Arduino for hardware.  Bosworth advocates for continuous experimentation and action to develop essential skills for the evolving tech landscape.

title: MS Dhoni not playing vs RCB: When could he return for CSK?
content: MS Dhoni will miss CSK’s IPL 2026 clash against RCB due to a calf strain, with the team struggling after two losses. While RCB enter confidently, CSK feel his absence. The franchise said he’ll miss two weeks, though coach Stephen Fleming stressed his strong off-field influence. Dhoni could return against KKR on April 14, or later fixtures if recovery takes longer.

title: Amid job cuts, Canadian billionaire Kevin O’Leary points out a good thing about AI layoffs
content: Kevin O'Leary, the Canadian billionaire, asserts that artificial intelligence is elevating human creativity, making artists and storytellers more valuable than ever. He highlights how compelling social media content, especially short-form videos, now commands significant salaries due to measurable customer acquisition. O'Leary believes AI is a powerful productivity tool, fostering new industries and opportunities across all economic sectors.

title: ‘Drugged, raped, forced into marriage': Hyderabad woman alleges coerced conversion
content: she provided an outline of a harrowing experience involving deception, physical violence, financial exploitation, and religious coercion. The victim met the accused, Nawaz, when he approached her to rent a shop. She alleged he falsely claimed to have converted to Hinduism and changed his name to Navadurga to gain her trust.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company hinted at current layoffs weeks in advance in Q3 earnings report
content: Oracle's Q3 FY2026 earnings release, filed the same week thousands of employees received termination emails, contained a quiet but telling admission: the company had already been restructuring its product development teams into smaller groups, using AI code generation to build software with fewer people. The layoffs, Oracle's largest-ever restructuring, hit teams across Oracle Health, Cloud, Sales, Customer Success, and NetSuite—with no prior warning to affected employees.

title: Suspected ISIS-linked terror module busted, youth radicalisation plot uncovered
content: Authorities have busted a terror network allegedly linked to ISIS and foreign handlers, accused of radicalizing Indian youth online. Teams across multiple states apprehended suspects involved in spreading jihadi propaganda, including videos of extremist figures. The group reportedly aimed to establish an Islamic State in India, with links traced to Pakistan, Afghanistan, Syria, and Bangladesh.

title: Tech blackout: Why UAE has pulled out from key Rafale deal
content: France will solely finance the upgraded F5 fighter jet program after UAE negotiations collapsed due to a lack of local industry involvement and technology access. This decision comes as France faces increasing defense pressures and competing priorities, highlighting Paris's commitment to maintaining sovereign control over its Rafale program.

title: Bangladesh cricket in turmoil: 4 directors resign, BCB chief says he'll be 'last to go'
content: Seven out of 25 directors have already stepped down, including four who resigned after Saturday’s board meeting. The exits have increased uncertainty around the future of the current administration.

title: Raghav Chadha made to stand like rooster, beaten in ‘secret mansion’: Ex-AAP leader
content: A political row has erupted within the party following allegations against Raghav Chadha. Chadha has vehemently denied these accusations, labeling them a coordinated campaign. This public exchange of claims signifies an escalating internal divide within the party.

title: No handshake! Uzbek GM stuns India's Harikai with 'Namaste' - Watch
content: At the Grenke Freestyle Open, Harika Dronavali offered a handshake while resigning, but Nodirbek Yakubboev responded with a respectful “Namaste,” both smiling. Meanwhile, Keymer and Niemann lead the event. Yakubboev earlier avoided shaking hands with Vaishali, later apologising with gifts. He clarified his actions were due to “religious reasons,” while expressing respect for her and Indian players.

title: ‘Bitter defeat for US’: Iran hails downing of F-15 as America rescues 2nd pilot
content: Iran claims a "bitter defeat" for the US after downing a jet and a C-130, while rescuing a downed F-15 crew member. President Trump, however, hailed the operation as a "daring" rescue, showcasing US "Air Dominance." The clash escalates regional tensions, disrupting vital aid and raising fears of widespread hunger.

title: Abhishek's season of ducks: SRH batter joins Rohit and Samson in unwanted list
content: Abhishek has had a slow start to IPL 2026, scoring 55 runs in three innings, with 48 of those coming against Kolkata Knight Riders in the previous match.

title: Congo to accept deportees from US under new deal with Trump admin
content: Democratic Republic of Congo will accept third-country nationals deported from the US starting April, a deal fully funded by Washington. This expands America's deportation network to Africa, with similar arrangements in Ghana and Cameroon.

title: 'Honour killing in Andhra Pradesh': Father confesses police helped track daughter
content: A father confessed to police that officers helped him locate his daughter, who he allegedly murdered for marrying against his wishes. The victim was handed over to her parents despite stating she was a major and wished to live with her husband. An investigation into the officers' conduct is expected.

title: UFC star Jon Jones accused of reckless driving in roadside clash, driver says let’s settle
content: Former two-division UFC champion Jon Jones was recently involved in a tense roadside confrontation in Albuquerque, New Mexico. The incident surfaced after a video was shared online by a local driver. He claimed that he was nearly hit multiple times by a black truck. What started as a frustrating moment on the road quickly escalated into a face-to-face exchange in a nearby parking lot.

title: Patna horror: Toddler gang-raped by uncle and two friends in field 500m from home
content: A horrific incident unfolded in Patna where a 22-year-old man allegedly gang-raped his three-year-old niece with two friends. The child's cries alerted locals, leading to the accused fleeing. Police swiftly apprehended two suspects, including the uncle, and are pursuing the third. The injured toddler is receiving medical care.

title: Big call! Ishan Kishan opts for Rs 13 crore weapon vs LSG
content: Lucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant won the toss and chose to bowl against Sunrisers Hyderabad. LSG made one change, bringing in Manimaran Siddharth for Anrich Nortje. SRH also introduced Liam Livingstone and Harshal Patel into their lineup, aiming for a crucial win after a mixed start to the season.

title: The 'difficult call' Riyan Parag took - and how it paid off for RR
content: Rajasthan Royals secured a thrilling six-run victory over Gujarat Titans, thanks to Riyan Parag's bold bowling decisions at the death. Parag's trust in Tushar Deshpande for the final over, after Jofra Archer's crucial 19th, paid off, boosting his confidence as a leader. Archer's economical over set the stage for Deshpande to defend 10 runs, securing the win.

title: 'Mischievous and must be opposed': Chidambaram slams Centre's move to convene Parliament at poll time
content: Congress leader P. Chidambaram criticized the Centre's decision to convene Parliament from April 16-18, just days before assembly elections in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal.

title: ‘Momos’, ‘Chinki’ slurs: Northeast dance troupe faces racial abuse at Patna station
content: A cultural dance troupe from Arunachal Pradesh faced racial harassment and was denied access to a washroom at Patna railway station. The incident, captured on video, shows a woman using derogatory slurs against the performers. Railway authorities intervened, and the ECR has urged passengers to treat everyone with respect.

title: Inside the world’s most in-demand restaurant, where tables sell out in 10 seconds!
content: Vikas Khanna's restaurant Bungalow-Ek Sau Ek in New York is a culinary sensation. Tables at this celebrated Indian restaurant are booked solid for weeks. Bookings open daily and vanish within seconds. Even former US President Barack Obama has sought a table. The restaurant offers a journey through India's diverse flavours and heritage.

title: Inside India's ghost malls: How nostalgic hangout spots lost their magic
content: India's once-vibrant malls are becoming 'ghost malls,' with nearly 20% underperforming or empty. This decline stems from poor planning, oversupply, and a failure to adapt to changing consumer experiences, not just e-commerce. While some struggle, these spaces offer potential for reinvention into entertainment, co-working, or educational hubs.

title: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge': Ranveer Singh starrer set to surpass Rs 1000cr in India
content: Witness the phenomenal rise of Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' as it takes the cinematic world by storm, breaking records and captivating audiences worldwide. This gripping espionage thriller has not only become the highest-grossing Indian film in North America but is also on the brink of crossing the Rs 1000 crore mark in India. Read on to know more in detail.

title: RCB vs CSK: How has Virat Kohli fared against Chennai in the IPL?
content: Virat Kohli continues his remarkable IPL journey, entering his 19th season with RCB and showcasing his dominance against Chennai Super Kings. He holds the record for most runs against CSK and is nearing another milestone, highlighting his consistent threat to the opposition.

title: 'Hate-spreading CM': Rahul Gandhi slams Himanta Biswa at Assam poll rally
content: Rahul Gandhi attacked Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma, labeling him the 'most corrupt and hate-spreading CM' and vowing to jail him. Gandhi also promised SC/ST status for six communities, higher tea worker wages, and monthly cash transfers for women if Congress forms the government in Assam.

title: Teachers beat 10-year-old in UP madrasa; one holds him down, another strikes with stick
content: Two Urdu teachers were arrested in Saharanpur, UP, after a video surfaced showing a 10-year-old student being beaten at a madrassa. The incident, which reportedly occurred eight months ago, led to the teachers' removal and subsequent arrest following a complaint and video evidence. Police are conducting a detailed investigation.

title: Strangled, body burnt to erase identity: Tattoo clue exposes killers; wife among 3 arrested in UP
content: Three individuals have been arrested in Agra for the alleged murder of Lokendra Singh, 39, who was strangled and then burned to conceal the crime. Police identified the victim based on a tattoo, uncovering a conspiracy involving his wife and her alleged lover, Mahesh Prajapati.

title: KVS admission result 2026: When will the first provisional list release? Check here
content: With registrations for KVS admissions 2026–27 now concluded, students and parents are awaiting the release of provisional selection lists scheduled in April. The first list for Balvatika classes will be declared on April 8, followed by Class I on April 9, with slight changes in select states. Additional lists are planned later in the month if seats remain vacant. Admissions for other classes and offline processes will also proceed through April as per the notified schedule.

title: LPG shortage: Campaigning at tea shops fails to gather steam in Kerala
content: Tea shops and restaurants, once vibrant hubs for socio-political discussions and campaign stops, are facing closures due to an LPG shortage. This scarcity is impacting candidates' ability to connect with diverse voters, as these casual hangouts are crucial for reaching a cross-section of the electorate during election campaigns.

content: Highlighting Ladakh's ongoing demands, he said the issue concerns the entire region and must be addressed collectively

content: Ahead of the launch of a 100-day action plan to conserve every drop of rainwater and increase groundwater levels, the Chief Minister calls for collective effort to provide irrigation facility to every acre in the State

content: Although the auction season commences in September–October and concludes by March every year, over 24 million kg of tobacco remains unsold on auction platforms in April this year

content: Michigan's 18-point margin of victory over Arizona is tied for the largest in an NCAA men's tournament game between 1-seeds since 1979.

title: Lendeborg hobbled, vows to play for U-M in final
content: Michigan star Yaxel Lendeborg suffered a left MCL sprain and left ankle sprain in Saturday's win over Arizona, but team trainer Chris Williams told ESPN he is "very confident" the forward will play in Monday's title game.

title: Geno apologizes to South Carolina for outburst
content: UConn coach Geno Auriemma apologized to the South Carolina staff for his emotional outburst at Gamecocks coach Dawn Staley in the closing seconds of their Final Four matchup.

title: Tracking fifth-year options for 2023 first-rounders: Who will return in 2027?
content: the deadline for teams to pick up fifth-year options on the rookie contracts of 2023 first-round draft picks is May 1 . if the team declines the option, the player remains under contract for 2027 . we are tracking the entire 2023 class, which had only 31 picks after the Miami Dolphins were stripped of their first round selection .

title: NBA playoff watch: Can ailing Lakers stay ahead of Nuggets?
content: the Los Angeles Lakers are still the 3-seed in the West, but that position could be rapidly slipping away . the Denver Nuggets will be without stars Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves for the remainder of the regular season . one of those games comes Thursday against the Golden State Warriors .

title: 117 wins? 40 different pitchers? Four themes that will dominate the Dodgers' regular season
content: the identity of the 2026 los Angeles Dodgers is presented in a 100-second hype video . it's set to the beat of Billie Eilish's "Bad Guy" and begins with a question, asked by actor Jason Bateman . "you need teams like us so you can point your finger and say, "That's the bad guy," he says .

title: NHL playoff watch: Where the wild cards stand on Sunday
content: the Nashville Predators reclaim the playoff position with a 6-3 win over the San Jose Sharks . the preds hold the second wild card with 81 points and 26 regulation wins through 76 games . they are five back of the Utah Mammoth for the first wild card .
